title: crepis meruensis
intro: Crepis meruensis, a member of the dandelion family (Asteraceae), is a flowering plant species native to the Meru region in Kenya. Its roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting digestive health, while its herbal preparations typically involve drying and crushing the roots and leaves to make teas, infusions, or capsules. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Crepis meruensis for medicinal use.
